name: Hypertrophic Osteodystrophy (HOD)
description: HOD is a painful developmental bone disease affecting rapidly growing large and giant breed puppies. It causes inflammation in the metaphyseal regions of long bones, leading to severe pain, fever, and lameness.
treatment_overview: Treatment involves NSAIDs, supportive care, and nutritional management. Severe cases may require corticosteroids. Most puppies recover but some have permanent limb deformities.
prevention_tips: Avoid over-supplementation with vitamins and minerals
